species: Microaerobic N2O-reducing chemostat enrichment consortium (dominated by uncultured Burkholderiales

growth_records.strain: Enrichment from Daejeon municipal WWTP anoxic activated sludge (December 2019 inoculum); reactor N2OR1
growth_records.medium: Modified MR-1 medium with 5.0 mM sodium acetate; N2O-fed (200 ppmv gas equilibration)

up.method: reported constant volumetric N2O reduction rate (headspace N2O sustained below detection)
O2 consumed (DO held ~9 µM by intrusion) but steady-state O2 consumption rate not quantified for N2OR1

sec.method: calculated from N2O reduction stoichiometry N2O -> N2 (1:1), = N2O consumption rate 0.13 µmol/L/h

growth_records.cite: Kim et al. Identification of nosZ-expressing microorganisms consuming trace N2O in microaerobic chemostat consortia dominated by an uncultured Burkholderiales. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-022-01260-5. [PMC9381517]
growth_records.snippet: Throughout 98 days of continuous operation, N2O concentration in the headspace was sustained below the detection limit, implying a constant N2O reduction rate of 0.13 µmol L−1 h−1. ...dissolved O2 concentration hovered between 8.0 µM and 10
growth_records.notes: Chemostat consortium (not a pure culture). At steady state, retained community grows at mu = dilution rate D = 0.026 h^-1 (paper: cells growing slower than D=0.026 h^-1 were washed out). Doubling time td = ln2/0.026 = 0.6931/0.026 = 26.66 h (calculated). Rates are VOLUMETRIC (µmol/L/h), NOT biomass-specific, because biomass (gDW) was not quantified — cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h. N2O reduction 0.13 µmol/L/h reported. Acetate uptake computed as D×Δacetate = 0.026×(5.0-4.15 mM)=22.1 µmol/L/h. N2 secretion inferred 1:1 from N2O reduction. Temperature 25 °C is inferred from the 25 °C used for all aqueous/gas Henry's-law conversions ('<49 nM in the aqueous phase at 25 °C'); not explicitly stated as incubation temperature. pH not stated (phosphate-buffered). Trace-metal (Table S1) and vitamin (Table S2) compositions are in supplementary files not available here.

growth_records.strain: Enrichment from Daejeon municipal WWTP anoxic activated sludge (July 2020 inoculum); reactor N2OR2
growth_records.medium: Modified MR-1 medium with 5.0 mM sodium acetate; N2O-fed (2000 ppmv gas equilibration)

up.method: reported steady-state volumetric N2O consumption rate (0.90 ± 0.04 µmol/L/h)
reported steady-state volumetric O2 consumption rate (14.3 ± 0.1 µmol/L/h)

sec.method: calculated from N2O reduction stoichiometry N2O -> N2 (1:1), = N2O consumption rate 0.90 µmol/L/h

growth_records.cite: Kim et al. Identification of nosZ-expressing microorganisms consuming trace N2O in microaerobic chemostat consortia dominated by an uncultured Burkholderiales. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-022-01260-5. [PMC9381517]
growth_records.snippet: Assuming that O2 penetration and N2O supply rates were constant..., the steady-state rates of O2 and N2O consumption were estimated as 14.3 ± 0.1 µmol L−1 h−1 and 0.90 ± 0.04 µmol L−1 h−1, respectively. Acetate concentrations in the reactor
growth_records.notes: Chemostat consortium (not pure culture). mu = D = 0.026 h^-1 at steady state (organisms slower than D washed out). td = ln2/0.026 = 26.66 h (calculated). Rates VOLUMETRIC (µmol/L/h), not biomass-specific (gDW not measured). Reported: O2 consumption 14.3 µmol/L/h and N2O consumption 0.90 µmol/L/h. Acetate uptake computed D×Δacetate = 0.026×(5.0-4.17 mM) = 21.6 µmol/L/h. N2 secretion inferred 1:1 from N2O reduction. N2OR2 differs from N2OR1 only in N2O feed (2000 vs 200 ppmv) and start-up (no initial batch); N2O consumed ~7-fold faster than N2OR1. Temperature 25 °C inferred from Henry's-law conversions; pH not stated. Trace metal/vitamin compositions in supplementary (not available).
